text:
Once upon a sun-drenched Saturday, in a quaint little town named Willow Hollow, lived a timid man named Bartholomew. Bartholomew didn't have many friends. He lived alone in a cozy cottage with big windows that looked out on the rolling green hills. Every day was almost the same: rise early, fetch a pot of chamomile tea, tidy up his garden, and putter around his workshop making... nothing much.

One day, while rummaging through an old trunk cluttered with dusty tomes, lost love letters, and a questionable collection of knickknacks, Bartholomew found something unusual—a letter from his beloved Rosa. She wrote about her travels and missives filled with longing. There were vivid descriptions of Parisian nights under cobblestones and gentle breezes through vineyards. Though Rosa lived miles away, the words had a magnetic pull.

Bartholomew crinkled the dusty page carefully and began to read aloud.
